name: Golden path: MSP discovers exposed client RDP and closes the gap
description: Board-ready story for a managed service provider (MSP): continuous assessment flags internet-reachable Remote Desktop on a labeled client workstation, ownership is clarified with the client's IT lead, corrective changes are tracked to completion, and post-change validation confirms the blast radius collapsed. Demonstration-grade synthetic telemetry only.

simulated_timeline.synthetic_narrative: SecureWatch correlates perimeter telemetry with CMDB context: workstation ACME-WIN-FIN03 (MSP-owned asset tag) exposes RDP-compatible listening behavior to commodity scan sources—no brute-force payloads, heuristic signal only.
Service delivery manager notifies the customer's IT owner; MSP policy requires VPN-only privileged access—the client acknowledges the exception that caused the posture drift.
Firewall rule simulation denies WAN→host RDP; host-based policy forces NLA-only where still required internally; MFA gate on privileged jump path recorded as satisfied in lab metadata.
Follow-up posture scan stub shows no reachable RDP from the synthetic internet perspective; auditor-friendly evidence bundle attached to tenant record.

payload.title: DEMO: Public RDP path on MSP-managed workstation (synthetic)

payload.summary: RDP WAN exposure removed; privileged access routed via MSP VPN posture (simulation)
payload.validation_note: Rescan shows no reachable RDP from external vantage (fixture)

events_emitted.headline: MSP perimeter intelligence observed exposure
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Signal is labeled as demo-only; links to client contract line and asset owner in CMDB stub.

events_emitted.headline: Normalized finding for customer leadership
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Severity and business impact summarized in MSP executive language for QBR-ready reporting.

events_emitted.headline: Remediation verified
events_emitted.synthetic_detail: Closed-loop evidence shows policy restored and stakeholder sign-off path satisfied in lab.

expected_agents_triggered.triggered_reason_synthetic: Perimeter-aligned exposure signal ingested with asset context.
High-impact exposure routes through IR orchestration and customer comms stubs.

expected_remediation.summary: Eliminate WAN RDP, enforce MSP VPN boundary, privileged access via MFA-protected bastion posture; archive validation scans for MSSP audit.

expected_autonomous_remediation.synthetic_action: Generate customer-facing remediation packet with SLA clock and milestone checklist.

expected_human_approval_gates.synthetic_rationale: MSP defers intrusive network moves to approved maintenance windows with customer CAB acknowledgment.

expected_final_report.title: Executive briefing — RDP exposure discovered and remediated under MSP governance
expected_final_report.synthetic_executive_summary: We detected an internet-reachable remote administration posture on an MSP-managed client asset, prioritized it with accountable owners, executed boundary-hardening changes in line with MSP policy, and closed the finding with repeatable validation artifacts suitable for SOC 2 and customer QBR narratives.

golden_path_demo.executive_timeline_summary: From first signal to validated closure inside a single MSP business day: discovery at T+0, client alignment inside the first operational hour, change execution within SLA for high exposures, independent-style validation documenting that the WAN attack surface for RDP collapsed.

expected_database_outputs.business_outcome: Risk item exists with MSP client attribution, severity, and traceable lifecycle through remediated.
Tamper-evident history for who saw the signal, who approved remediation, and when validation completed.
Work-items map to SLA timers and MSP billing/PSA-friendly references (demo stub).
Decision engine rationale stored for MSSP auditors (fail-open/fail-closed posture per tenant controls).

expected_dashboard_summary.headline: High-risk exposure surfaced and remediated faster than SLA — customer-visible proof
kpis: Time-to-acknowledged (executive metric): trending under MSP Gold-tier target for critical exposure class
Open critical exposures attributable to WAN RDP posture: trending to zero across monitored estate (demo cohort)
Customer satisfaction proxy (CSAT) retained on playbook communication quality (synthetic rollup)
Repeat drift guardrails: MFA + VPN prerequisite compliance rate improved post-change
Audit readiness: SOC 2 / CMMC evidence packets auto-staged for MSSP auditors
expected_dashboard_summary.narrative: Leadership sees a single-pane story: exposure detected, stakeholder loop closed, corrective work packaged with evidence—not a toolchain demo. Charts emphasize reduction in unmanaged attack surface and predictable MSP operating rhythm.
